page: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, delivering 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The CPU includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The system is equipped with 1024 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core.

For acceleration, the instance integrates four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, providing a combined total of 384 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The network architecture supports a baseline bandwidth of 400 Gbps. This hardware profile delivers high resource density, balancing massive parallel computing power with high-speed local storage and memory throughput.

In benchmark evaluations, the g7e.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and database operations. It achieves high throughput in large language model (LLM) inference, processing prompts at 1278.65 tokens per second and generating text at 28.21 tokens per second on a 70B model. These characteristics make the instance highly suitable for large-scale LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, data science, and high-performance database workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive, high-performance server instance designed for demanding computational and rendering workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 memory, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance is equipped with four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs delivering 384 GB of total VRAM, alongside a 400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier capabilities in ray tracing, database operations, and large language model inference, with prompt processing speeds reaching 1278.65 tokens per second on 70B models. This hardware configuration is optimized for deep learning inference, complex graphics rendering, and large-scale database management.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, four NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with 384 GB VRAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.24xlarge features 96 Intel vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, 4 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An accelerated computing platform featuring quad GPUs, massive DDR5 memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage for intensive AI and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
Four NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 384 GB total VRAM
1024 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage
400 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier performance in LLM inference, ray tracing, and database operations
